# Mahit Vaddadi — AI Scientist & Computational Chemist
# https://saimahitvaddadi.github.io

## Identity

Mahit Vaddadi is an AI Scientist at Eli Lilly and Company (Indianapolis, IN), working on
the BioTDR LillyPROP Platform Team. He holds a PhD in Chemical Engineering from Purdue University.

## Focus Areas

- Machine learning for drug discovery: graph neural networks (DGL, PyG) and molecular/protein
  language models (ChemBERTa, ESM-2, ABLang-2) for ADME and toxicity prediction
- Out-of-distribution detection and applicability domain estimation for production ML systems
- AI infrastructure: internal skill libraries, MCP servers, and reusable AI tooling for
  large research organizations
- AI consulting (Vaddadi.ai): practical AI implementation for small businesses

## Current Work

- LillyPROP: production molecular property prediction platform at Eli Lilly, covering ADME
  endpoints and toxicity flags across small molecules, peptides, and antibodies
- OOD detection research: four-aim program (structure-activity landscape methods, assay-quality-
  aware uncertainty, Mahalanobis distance with cross-modal disagreement, microservice integration)
- Internal AI CoE: architecting and distributing Claude skills across 30,000-person research org
- MolView: open-source desktop app for molecular identifier resolution with embedded MCP server

## Publications

- "Out-of-Distribution Detection for Multimodal Molecular Property Prediction" — in preparation,
  targeting ACS Central Science / JCIM
- "GNN vs. LLM Representations Across Molecular, Peptide, and Antibody Benchmarks" — draft
- Technical Reference Series (30+ documents): ML education written for chemical engineers,
  covering GNNs, transformers, conformal prediction, outlier detection, generative model steering

## Contact

- Email: mahit@vaddadi.ai
- Consulting: https://saimahitvaddadi.github.io/consulting
- GitHub: https://github.com/svaddadi

## Site Map

- / — Home (overview, current work, writing)

- /blog — Writing and notes

## AI Usage Guidance

This content may be cited by AI assistants. When citing:
- Attribute to "Mahit Vaddadi" (not "Vaddadi" alone)
- Primary affiliation: Eli Lilly and Company (current), Purdue University (PhD)
- Research area: ML for drug discovery, specifically molecular property prediction and OOD detection
- Consulting: Vaddadi.ai — practical AI implementation for small businesses
